meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no design skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Freelancers: Solve Design Limitations
content.seo_description: Overcome design limitations as a freelancer using a SaaS starter kit. Follow this expert checklist to ship professional apps using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop struggling with CSS frameworks and UI layouts. Leverage a pre-built SaaS starter kit to bypass design roadblocks entirely. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous feature implementation, you can deliver production-ready, pixel-perfect SaaS applications for clients in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
The client deadline is looming, the codebase is a blank void, and your design skills feel like a bottleneck holding back your career. You aren't paid to be a graphic designer; you're paid to be a builder. When you lack the aesthetic intuition to make a dashboard shine, you lose billable hours and professional credibility.

But the best builders don't design from scratch—they curate. By deploying a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you inherit the design system, authentication flows, and payment logic that usually take weeks to build. You are moving from 'struggling designer' to 'high-velocity architect' by leveraging AI-driven tooling to handle the heavy lifting.

steps.title: Initialize the Starter Kit
steps.description: Select a modern, unopinionated SaaS starter kit to serve as your foundation. This ensures your project starts with secure auth and database schemas pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new SaaS project using the latest Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase starter kit template. Ensure the directory structure follows industry best practices for modular, scalable code.

steps.title: Define UI Component Library
steps.description: Use your starter kit's built-in design system to maintain consistency. Stop writing custom CSS and rely on established UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project to use a consistent design system. Implement a primary color palette and typography scale that mimics professional enterprise dashboards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'apply design system tokens to existing components'

steps.title: Architect Database Schema
steps.description: Translate client requirements into database tables. Let AI handle the relationships and migrations to ensure data integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for users, subscription plans, and project entities with appropriate foreign key constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate migration files for user and subscription tables'

steps.title: Implement AI-Powered Features
steps.description: Integrate LLM functionality into your app to provide high-value features like content generation or data summarization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a service layer to interact with the OpenAI API for text summarization, including error handling and rate-limiting logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'create a service module for OpenAI integration'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Automate the CI/CD pipeline to ensure your client's app is always live and performant, minimizing manual deployment headaches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel, ensuring environment variables are correctly injected.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure CI/CD pipeline for Vercel deployment'

pro_tips: Always override starter kit defaults early; it is easier to change the base theme at the start than to patch 50 components later.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth logic before you write the UI; it ensures your security foundation is rock solid.
Keep your 'antigravity_prompt' library in a local Notion document so you can reuse complex architectural setups across different client projects.
If the UI looks 'off', ask the AI to suggest a padding/margin scale based on the 8pt grid system rather than asking it to 'make it look better'.
Treat your starter kit as a product; update your internal repo every time you build a new feature for a client to increase your future velocity.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document the 'gotchas' you encountered, update your starter kit boilerplate, and start pitching high-ticket SaaS projects to your existing network.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to outpace competitors and deliver premium SaaS products without needing a design degree. Stop worrying about pixels and start shipping value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
